Off Calendar May-22-2017 Text Ruling

Hearing On Fee Waiver Request.
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-29-2017 Continued To May-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Griffin (Administrator).
Hannah Salassi for and with Pamela Brown (Objector).
John Shafai appeared by telephone for Stone Street Services.
Toby Merchant (Brother of Decedent) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Shafai stated that his Client's position has always been that they own all of the annuity payments and that he does not want to litigate the case because they have a settlement with the Administrator. Ms. Harrington clarified that there is no settlement between Ms. Brown and Stone Street. The Amended Petition to Determine Heirship, First Account and Report of Administrator, and to Determine that Pamela Brown is Not Entitled to Any Share of the John Hancock Annuity is continued to May 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a half day (9:00 to 12:00) evidentiary hearing on the issue of abandonment. The parties agree that no more evidence is to be provided on the issue of the waiver. The Parties are to meet and confer up to that date. The Court is to receive witness lists, exhibit lists, trial briefs and stipulations by May 2, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2017 Continued To Mar-29-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Continued To Mar-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Griffin.
Hannah Salassi for and with Pamela Brown.
John Safai appeared by telephone for Stone Street Services.
Toby Merchant in Propria Persona.

Ms. Harrington stated that objection was filed after the deadline, that it was not served on anyone and that she needs time to respond to the objection. Ms. Salassi stated that the implication of the waiver and the prior criminal proceeding should be resolved with briefs, and that the potential issue would be about abandonment. Ms. Harrington stated that she will amend the petition to address the issue of heirship separately. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to March 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Barrientos. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-18-2017 Continued To Feb-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Griffin (Administrator).
Hannah Salassi specially appearing for and with Pamela Brown.
John Shafai appeared by telephone for Stone Street Services.
Toby Merchant in Propria Persona.

Ms. Salassi stated that her office was recently contacted by Ms. Brown, that her office has not had a chance to review the matter and that they are not in a position to determine whether they will take the case. The First Account; Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to February 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Brown that this will be the end of it and that the matter will be submitted with or without objections. An appearance will be required on that date. Objections are to be filed no later than January 30, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Barrientos. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Continued To Jan-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Griffin (Administrator).
John Shafai appeared by telephone for Stone Street Services.
Pamela Brown in Propria Persona.

Ms. Brown explained that she has been in search of an Attorney and has spoken with several, that some of them would not take the case, that some were charging too much and that she received a contract from one of them, but she does not agree with the contract. Ms. Brown also requested another continuance stating that she has provided documents to one Attorney and is waiting to hear back from her. Ms. Harrington argued that Ms. Brown has had since September to seek Counsel, spoke of Ms. Brown's relationship with the Brothers of the Decedent and raised the issue of Ms. Brown's abandonment of her Sons. The first account and petition for final distribution is continued to January 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for one last time. The Court informed Ms. Brown that it will make a decision on that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Continued To Jan-04-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for (administrator) Franza Giffin. John Shafai (via court call) for Stone Street Services. Pamela Brown in Propria Persona. Toby Merchant also present.

Ms. Harrington reported that she filed a supplement on November 23, 2016, that the only issue is if Ms. Brown is entitled to a share, that she filed a waiver of rights which qualifies as a 275 disclaimer, that it indicates "any and all benefits now and forever", presented the history of why the waiver came to be, that Ms. Brown abandoned her children and per the code does not inherit and that they will be filing a declaration regarding the abandonment. Mr. Shafai stated that he agrees with counsel, that the annuity policy is a settlement of the insurance claim and a benefit that Ms. Brown waived and that if she is awarded anything there will be further litigation and the beneficiaries will lose the settlement. Ms. Brown clarified that her name is Pamela Brown and not Pamela Weston, that the waiver and so called abandonment is false, that even though she was in jail for murder she was found not guilty due to stupidity of not knowing what was going on, that she may have signed papers regarding her sons getting the annuity but whatever is being told to them is false. The Court asked Ms. Brown if she is officially contesting the matter. Ms. Brown stated that she is and that she is the mother, that she does not see why they cannot share what her deceased son left, confirmed that she would like time to find counsel but that it has been hard to retain an attorney due to the information they are receiving and that her sons think that she contacted an attorney to get all of the money. Ms. Harrington stated that in regards to the underlying lawsuit the claims were settled with Ms. Brown signing the waiver, that they would not have paid anything out to Mr. Brown and requested that the continuance be short as Ms. Brown has been aware of this. Mr. Shafai stated that all of the rights were sold for a lump sum settlement and that sharing the money with Ms. Brown will put the whole settlement in jeopardy. The Court continued the petition for final distribution to January 4, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and stated that Ms. Brown needs to have counsel present or the matter will be submitted as of that date, that any filings are due at least one week prior to the hearing date and that Mr. Merchant is to wait for everything to be filed and then respond.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Continued To Nov-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Giffen. John Shafai (via court call) for Stone Street Services. Toby Merchant also present.

The Court asked about responses to the examiner's notes. Ms. Harrington stated that she filed responses, gave a history of the case, that the insurance company refused to honor the policies until the children sued them, that Pamela Weston waived all rights to the payout due to charges against her as co-conspirator, that the only asset of the estate is the annuity which she has waived rights to, that Ms. Weston received notice of the proceedings, that Ms. Weston told her attorney as well as Toby that she waived her rights to the payout, that the estate is to be split 50/50 between Stone Street Services and Charles Jr. & Toby Merchant. Mr. Shafai stated that he has nothing to add. Mr. Merchant stated that his mother wants no part of this. Ms. Harrington provided the Court with background on Ms. Weston subsequent to the murder and read Ms. Weston's waiver to the Court. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Compromise Of Litigation
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for (administrator) Franza Giffin. Deepak Singh for Travelers Insurance.

The Court stated that this is a petition to approve a compromise, that it is curious as the administrator is not a party to the litigation or a party to the agreement and asked why here and now. Ms. Harrington reported that the settlement of the litigation was by the parties, that it could not have been done prior to the appointment, that all parties agree that this is the right place to obtain and order regarding resolution, that in regards to value to the estate this came from old litigation in the 1980's, that it is a good settlement for the estate and that they worry that the estate will lose if it is not acted on. The Court stated that it has concerns regarding what the mother will do. Ms. Harrington stated that the mother has had every opportunity to object but has not and that they will later have to determine who is entitled to the estate. Mr. Singh that he is here on behalf of Traveler's Insurance and that they are just trying to find out where to pay the annuity. The Court stated that all it is being asked to do is say that the agreement is approved and allowed. Ms. Harrington stated that Stone Street has not served the administrator and that she could object. The Court asked if the order extinguishes the claim and that this only approves the agreement by the parties. Ms. Harrington stated that the estate feels comfortable moving forward with a court approved agreement and that they have come to a good resolution for all parties. The Court took the petition for order approving compromise of litigation under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Giffen. John Shafai (via court call) for Stone Street Services.

Mr. Shafai stated that he has no objections or arguments and that they have a settlement agreement with decedents brother. The court asked if any conflicts were anticipated down the road. Ms. Harrington stated no, that there was litigation regarding the annuity that has been resolved, that part of the resolution resolved how payments would be provided and that regarding their interest it is already settled. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Shafai (via court call) for petitioner. Amy Harrington for (brothers of decedent) Charles & Toby Merchant.

Ms. Harrington reported that she filed a petition on Friday (case PTR-15-298750), that in the early 1980's there was a minor's compromise, a trust was issued, that she has filed a 17200 regarding annuity payments on the trust, that she has been speaking with Mr. Shafai and wants the opportunity to put forward the correct interpretation. The Court stated its concern with having a creditor as a personal representative and that it was anticipating a petition being filed by Ms. Harrington's clients. Ms. Harrington stated that the annuity payments are not part of the estate, not subject to court supervision and that Mr. Shafai feels that he purchased them. Mr. Shafai stated that he is in agreement with Ms. Harrington, that the insurance company stated that this would have to go to probate and that he would agree to a continuance in this case to see what happens in the trust matter. Ms. Harrington stated that she would object to the creditors serving and that she does not think there is anything in the estate. The Court took the Petition for Letters of Administration under submission and stated that Mr. Shafai can file something if he does not think the July 13th hearing in the trust matter will produce a remedy and that he will not rule on this for one week.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Continued To May-11-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Shafai (via court call) for the (Petitioner) Stone Street Services, Inc. Pamela Brown (mother) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Shafai reported that he has spoken with Amy Harrington who is representing the brothers Charles Merchant & Toby Merchant, that they may want to serve as administrator and that they have agreed to a 30 day continuance. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to May 11, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
